Home
====

!Photo of Yoshua Bengio

A.M. Turing Award Recipient and most-cited AI researcher
--------------------------------------------------------

**Yoshua Bengio** is Full Professor of Computer Science at [Université de Montreal](https://www.umontreal.ca/), Co-President and Scientific Director of [LawZero](http://lawzero.org/), as well as the Founder and Scientific Advisor of [Mila](http://mila.quebec/). He also holds a Canada [CIFAR](https://cifar.ca/) AI Chair. Considered one of the world’s leaders in Artificial Intelligence and Deep Learning, he is the recipient of the 2018 A.M. Turing Award, considered to be the “Nobel Prize of computing.” He is the most cited computer scientist worldwide, and the most-cited living scientist across all fields (by total citations).

Professor Bengio is a Fellow of both the Royal Society of London and Canada, an Officer of the Order of Canada, a Knight of the Legion of Honor of France, a member of the UN’s Scientific Advisory Board for Independent Advice on Breakthroughs in Science and Technology, and chairs the International AI Safety Report.

News

!
!

!International AI Safety Report

### 2026 International AI Safety Report

The International AI Safety Report is the most comprehensive evidence-based assessment of AI capabilities, emerging risks, and safety measures to date, with contributions from more than 100 independent experts nominated by over 30 countries and international organisations, including the EU, OECD, and UN.

<https://internationalaisafetyreport.org/>

!LawZero 

### Scientist AI Blog Post

At LawZero, we're rethinking the building blocks of frontier AI to create an intelligent machine that is both highly capable and safe-by-design. Learn more in our first blog post outlining some of the objectives and core components of our Scientist AI project.

<https://lawzero.org/en/unlisted/scientist-ai-safe-design-not-desiring>

![Yoshua Bengio TED Talk](https://yoshuabengio.org/sites/default/files/styles/video_thumbnail_1280x720/public/blocks/slider/1/ted2025202504071jr4173-medium.jpg?itok=Id9hkLQ7)

!play

### TED Talk, April 2025

When I realized how dangerous the current agency-driven AI trajectory could be for future generations, I knew I had to do all I could to make AI safer. In this TED Talk, I shared this personal experience, and outlined the scientific solution I envision.

<https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qe9QSCF-d88>

###### Recent Blog Posts

### Introducing LawZero

I am launching a new non-profit AI safety research organization called LawZero, to prioritize safety over commercial imperatives.

Published

03 June 2025

By

Yoshua Bengio

### Implications of Artificial General Intelligence on National and International Security

As highlighted in the International Scientific Report on the Safety of Advanced AI, the capabilities of general-purpose AI systems have been steadily increasing over the last decade, with a pronounced acceleration in the last few years.

Published

30 October 2024

By

Yoshua Bengio

### Bounding the probability of harm from an AI to create a guardrail

As we move towards more powerful AI, it becomes urgent to better understand the risks, ideally in a mathematically rigorous and quantifiable way, and use that knowledge to mitigate them.

Published

29 August 2024

By

Yoshua Bengio
